title: Simulation Code and Data for:  Numerical Analysis of Temporal Correlations and Hong–Ou–Mandel Interference in SPDC
author: Albashir, Ahmed
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This repository contains the Python source code and simulation framework used to investigate non-local temporal correlations in Spontaneous Parametric Down-Conversion (SPDC). The core feature of this framework is the implementation of a computationally generated "Digital Twin" protocol, which establishes a separable baseline state to isolate and quantify entanglement-induced effects during Hong-Ou-Mandel (HOM) interference. The provided scripts encompass the full analytical pipeline, including phase-matching data generation, complex Joint Spectral Amplitude (JSA) matrix construction, numerical Schmidt decomposition, and the extraction of temporal broadening factors.
steps_raw: This repository contains the Python source code and simulation framework used to investigate non-local temporal correlations in Spontaneous Parametric Down-Conversion (SPDC). The core feature of this framework is the implementation of a computationally generated "Digital Twin" protocol, which establishes a separable baseline state to isolate and quantify entanglement-induced effects during Hong-Ou-Mandel (HOM) interference. The provided scripts encompass the full analytical pipeline, including phase-matching data generation, complex Joint Spectral Amplitude (JSA) matrix construction, numerical Schmidt decomposition, and the extraction of temporal broadening factors.
